title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.
writeup: Anthropic's Code with Claude Tokyo keynote announces Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, the fifth generation of Claude models, and walks through why Fable 5 marks a step change for agentic software engineering. The talk pairs model capability claims (SWE-bench Pro leadership, long-horizon autonomy spanning millions of tokens, single-shot correctness on complex tasks) with a live account of the safety trade-off that lets Fable 5 ship broadly while Mythos 5 stays restricted to Project Glasswing partners. A secondary thread covers new Claude Managed Agents platform features, including scheduled deployments and secrets vaults, and Claude Code usage reaching 20 hours per week per developer.
key_points: Fable 5 leads SWE-bench Pro, and the gap over competing models widens as task length and complexity increase, because single-shot correctness and long-horizon coherence (across millions of tokens, running for days on a single goal) are its two primary differentiators.
Fable 5 dispatches and supervises sub-agents more reliably and with greater cost-consciousness than any prior Claude model, making multi-agent orchestration at scale practical for production teams.

A dual-model safeguard system routes Fable 5 requests that touch cybersecurity, biology, or chemistry topics to Opus 4.8 instead; the response is labeled and billed at Opus prices, allowing the high-capability model to ship broadly rather than stay gated for months.

Mythos 5 is the same underlying model as Fable 5 but with the cyber and biosecurity safeguards lifted; it is available today only to Project Glasswing partners, with broader life-sciences researcher access beginning later in the month.
Claude Managed Agents now supports scheduled agent deployments (run agents on any cadence) and secrets vaults (agents make authenticated API calls without receiving the raw keys directly).

Rakuten reduced its major release cadence from once per quarter to every two weeks by moving from Claude Code-assisted development to Claude Managed Agents powering autonomous teams across engineering, product, sales, and finance.
